beats.activity: Wake up, check tide tables and weather, make coffee and breakfast.
beats.reasoning: Woke early yesterday to check tides—that's a pattern. Rainy morning means he'll check conditions before committing to fieldwork. Low energy (35/100) suggests a slower start, but tide-checking is automatic for a marine biologist.

beats.activity: Commute to Capitol Hill office via Interbay, avoiding SR-520 eastbound closure.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound is closed 9am–noon; Liam would naturally avoid it. Takes the scenic Interbay route along the water instead of fighting traffic—fits his tendency to take the scenic route and mood-based planning.

beats.activity: Check lab equipment, review sample data from yesterday, plan fieldwork for the afternoon if conditions permit.
beats.reasoning: Rain until 2pm means he can't get out on the Sound until late morning at the earliest. Uses morning for lab work and planning. Low energy suggests he's not pushing hard yet.

beats.activity: Grab a coffee and pastry at a local cafe while the rain continues.
beats.reasoning: He grabbed breakfast at Molly Moon's yesterday—it's clearly a go-to spot. Rainy morning is perfect for lingering over coffee. Skeptical Seattleite doesn't mind the rain; it's part of the rhythm.

beats.activity: Back to the lab—prep sampling gear, check boat fuel and weather radar for a 1pm departure.
beats.reasoning: Rain is scheduled to end by 2pm. Fieldwork on Puget Sound is his main job; he's preparing to get out on the water once conditions improve. Boat is likely moored in Ballard.

beats.activity: Drive to Ballard to meet the team and prep the boat.
beats.reasoning: Same route as yesterday—Capitol Hill to Ballard for the boat. Rain is still steady but easing. Budget enough time to gear up before heading out onto the Sound.

beats.activity: Final gear check, safety briefing, cast off from Ballard boat launch.
beats.reasoning: Rain is ending around 2pm, but conditions should be workable by noon. He wants to maximize time on the water while the ecosystem is still active. Team coordination is part of the job.

beats.activity: Out on Puget Sound collecting samples, observing kelp forest and marine life.
beats.reasoning: Core fieldwork. Yesterday was a great day; today he's chasing similar conditions. Rain has cleared by early afternoon. Four-and-a-half hours gives him a solid sampling window before heading back.

beats.activity: Return to Ballard boat launch, secure the boat, rinse and stow gear.
beats.reasoning: Standard post-fieldwork routine. Gear needs to be properly stored. Low energy (35/100) means he's not pushing for extra tasks—just the essentials.

beats.activity: Grab an early dinner at a neighborhood spot in Ballard, maybe Thai or casual seafood.
beats.reasoning: Post-work meal before heading home. Ballard has good food options. He's been active all day and needs to refuel. Keeps it simple—no big crowd.

beats.activity: Drive home to Madrona along Lake Washington Boulevard.
beats.reasoning: He took the scenic lake route yesterday evening and loved it. Fits his personality—takes the scenic route. Evening light should be nice despite the earlier rain.

world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
